operation_family_ledger.note: Per-projection timing not instrumented. Available: per-layer graph_us + eval_us only.
operation_family_ledger.projection_families: not_instrumented — estimated 59.8% of wall time based on layer composition

memory_bound_proof.note: Model fits in memory via mapped-no-copy. All 1180 tensors mapped directly from mmap'd segments. No weight copies.

t2_probe.reason: External array dtype mismatch — Uint8 external arrays report as Uint32 through MLX Metal path. Copy comparison could not execute.

conclusion: Decode bottleneck confirmed: eval_us (Metal kernel + sync) dominates at 74.7% of wall time.
Graph construction (lazy MLX operation recording) accounts for 16.2%.
Per-projection timing NOT available — cannot determine which matmul dominates.
T2 contiguity hypothesis UNTESTED due to external array dtype mismatch.
No evidence of hidden weight copies at model load (all 1180 tensors mapped).
No evidence of RSS growth during decode (stable at ~16MB after initial layers).
Full attention layers are 1.4x more expensive than sliding per layer.
KV cache copies only for full attention (16KB/step/layer × 8 layers).

authorization_detail.layout_qualification: NOT AUTHORIZED — per-projection timing unavailable; T2 probe blocked
authorization_detail.kv_optimization: NOT AUTHORIZED — KV copy is only 131KB total, overshadowed by eval time
authorization_detail.graph_sync_optimization: NOT AUTHORIZED — graph is only 16.2%; eval dominates
authorization_detail.residency_optimization: NOT AUTHORIZED — model fits in memory via mapped-no-copy; RSS stable
authorization_detail.mlx_kernel_path_investigation: AUTHORIZED — eval dominates at 74.7%; per-projection timing is prerequisite
authorization_detail.next_action: Add per-qmatmul (per-projection) timing instrumentation to executor.rs. Instrument each quantized_matmul call with elapsed time. Re-run decode-one. Then re-evaluate layout qualification.
